audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for second divine court scene, ha-satan as the Accuser, skin-for-skin idiom, bodily affliction, Job's spouse, Job's refusal to sin with lips, and friends' silent mourning.

verses.restored: Again there was a day when the sons of the Divine came to present themselves before the Eternal, and the Accuser also came among them to present himself before the Eternal.
The Eternal said to the Accuser, From where do you come? The Accuser answered the Eternal and said, From roaming through the earth and walking around in it.

The Eternal said to the Accuser, Have you set your heart on my servant Job? For there is none like him on the earth, a whole and upright man, fearing the Divine and turning away from evil. He still holds fast to his integrity, though you incited me against him to swallow him up for nothing.
The Accuser answered the Eternal and said, Skin for skin; all that a man has he will give for his life.
But stretch out your hand now and touch his bone and his flesh, and surely he will bless/curse you to your face.
The Eternal said to the Accuser, Look, he is in your hand; only guard his life.
The Accuser went out from the presence of the Eternal and struck Job with severe sores from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.
He took a potsherd to scrape himself while he sat among the ashes.
His woman said to him, Do you still hold fast to your integrity? Bless/curse the Divine and die.
He said to her, You speak as one of the foolish women speaks. Shall we receive good from the Divine and not also receive harm? In all this Job did not sin with his lips.

Three friends of Job heard of all this harm that had come upon him. Each came from his place: Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. They agreed together to come to show sympathy to him and to comfort him.
They lifted their eyes from afar and did not recognize him. They lifted their voice and wept; each tore his robe and threw dust toward the heavens over his head.
They sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and no one spoke a word to him, for they saw that the pain was very great.
